heading: Methane emissions and waste reduction incentive program for petroleum and natural gas systems
label: 7436 Methane emissions and waste reduction incentive program for petroleum and natural gas systems
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (g). substituted “calendar year 2034” for “calendar year 2024”.Pub. L. 119–21
amendments: Amendments2025—Subsec. (g). substituted “calendar year 2034” for “calendar year 2024”.Pub. L. 119–21

children.heading: Incentives for methane mitigation and monitoring
children.label: a Incentives for methane mitigation and monitoring

children.text: for grants, rebates, contracts, loans, and other activities of the Environmental Protection Agency for the purposes of providing financial and technical assistance to owners and operators of applicable facilities to prepare and submit greenhouse gas reports under subpart W of part 98 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations;

for grants, rebates, contracts, loans, and other activities of the Environmental Protection Agency authorized under subsections (a) through (c) of for methane emissions monitoring;section 7403 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: improving climate resiliency of communities and petroleum and natural gas systems;

children.label: B
children.text: improving and deploying industrial equipment and processes that reduce methane and other greenhouse gas emissions and waste;

children.label: C
children.text: supporting innovation in reducing methane and other greenhouse gas emissions and waste from petroleum and natural gas systems;

children.label: D
children.text: permanently shutting in and plugging wells on non-Federal land;

children.label: E
children.text: mitigating health effects of methane and other greenhouse gas emissions, and legacy air pollution from petroleum and natural gas systems in low-income and disadvantaged communities; and

children.label: F
children.text: supporting environmental restoration; and
to cover all direct and indirect costs required to administer this section, prepare inventories, gather empirical data, and track emissions.

children.heading: Incentives for methane mitigation from conventional wells
children.label: b Incentives for methane mitigation from conventional wells
children.text: In addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated to the Administrator for fiscal year 2022, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $700,000,000, to remain available until , for activities described in paragraphs (1) through (4) of subsection (a) at marginal conventional wells.September 30, 2028

children.heading: Waste emissions charge
children.label: c Waste emissions charge
children.text: The Administrator shall impose and collect a charge on methane emissions that exceed an applicable waste emissions threshold under subsection (f) from an owner or operator of an applicable facility that reports more than 25,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent of greenhouse gases emitted per year pursuant to subpart W of part 98 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations, regardless of the reporting threshold under that subpart.
Offshore petroleum and natural gas production.
Onshore petroleum and natural gas production.
Onshore natural gas processing.
Onshore natural gas transmission compression.
Underground natural gas storage.
Liquefied natural gas storage.
Liquefied natural gas import and export equipment.
Onshore petroleum and natural gas gathering and boosting.
Onshore natural gas transmission pipeline.

the number of metric tons of methane emissions reported pursuant to subpart W of part 98 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations, for the applicable facility that exceed the applicable annual waste emissions threshold listed in subsection (f) during the previous reporting period; and

children.label: A
children.text: $900 for emissions reported for calendar year 2024;

children.label: B
children.text: $1,200 for emissions reported for calendar year 2025; or

children.label: C
children.text: $1,500 for emissions reported for calendar year 2026 and each year thereafter.

children.label: A
children.text: 0.20 percent of the natural gas sent to sale from such facility; or

children.label: B
children.text: 10 metric tons of methane per million barrels of oil sent to sale from such facility, if such facility sent no natural gas to sale.

children.heading: Nonproduction petroleum and natural gas systems
children.label: 2 Nonproduction petroleum and natural gas systems
children.text: With respect to imposing and collecting the charge under subsection (c) for an applicable facility in an industry segment listed in paragraph (3), (6), (7), or (8) of subsection (d), the Administrator shall impose and collect the charge on the reported metric tons of methane emissions that exceed 0.05 percent of the natural gas sent to sale from or through such facility.

children.heading: Natural gas transmission
children.label: 3 Natural gas transmission
children.text: With respect to imposing and collecting the charge under subsection (c) for an applicable facility in an industry segment listed in paragraph (4), (5), or (9) of subsection (d), the Administrator shall impose and collect the charge on the reported metric tons of methane emissions that exceed 0.11 percent of the natural gas sent to sale from or through such facility.

children.heading: Common ownership or control
children.label: 4 Common ownership or control
children.text: In calculating the total emissions charge obligation for facilities under common ownership or control, the Administrator shall allow for the netting of emissions by reducing the total obligation to account for facility emissions levels that are below the applicable thresholds within and across all applicable segments identified in subsection (d).

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: 5 Exemption
children.text: Charges shall not be imposed pursuant to paragraph (1) on emissions that exceed the waste emissions threshold specified in such paragraph if such emissions are caused by unreasonable delay, as determined by the Administrator, in environmental permitting of gathering or transmission infrastructure necessary for offtake of increased volume as a result of methane emissions mitigation implementation.

children.label: i
children.text: methane emissions standards and plans pursuant to subsections (b) and (d) of have been approved and are in effect in all States with respect to the applicable facilities; andsection 7411 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: compliance with the requirements described in clause (i) will result in equivalent or greater emissions reductions as would be achieved by the proposed rule of the Administrator entitled “Standards of Performance for New, Reconstructed, and Modified Sources and Emissions Guidelines for Existing Sources: Oil and Natural Gas Sector Climate Review” (86 Fed. Reg. 63110 ()), if such rule had been finalized and implemented.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Resumption of charge
children.label: B Resumption of charge
children.text: If the conditions in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A) cease to apply after the Administrator has made the determination in that subparagraph, the applicable facility will again be subject to the charge under subsection (c) beginning in the first calendar year in which the conditions in either clause (i) or (ii) of that subparagraph are no longer met.

children.heading: Plugged wells
children.label: 7 Plugged wells
children.text: Charges shall not be imposed with respect to the emissions rate from any well that has been permanently shut-in and plugged in the previous year in accordance with all applicable closure requirements, as determined by the Administrator.

children.heading: Period
children.label: g Period
children.text: The charge under subsection (c) shall be imposed and collected beginning with respect to emissions reported for calendar year 2034 and for each year thereafter.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: h Reporting
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Administrator shall revise the requirements of subpart W of part 98 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations, to ensure the reporting under such subpart, and calculation of charges under subsections (e) and (f) of this section, are based on empirical data, including data collected pursuant to subsection (a)(4), accurately reflect the total methane emissions and waste emissions from the applicable facilities, and allow owners and operators of applicable facilities to submit empirical emissions data, in a manner to be prescribed by the Administrator, to demonstrate the extent to which a charge under subsection (c) is owed.August 16, 2022

children.heading: Definition of greenhouse gas
children.label: i Definition of greenhouse gas
children.text: In this section, the term “greenhouse gas” means the air pollutants carbon dioxide, hydrofluorocarbons, methane, nitrous oxide, perfluorocarbons, and sulfur hexafluoride.
